title: Official International Reserves
summary: The Bank of Canada released weekly international reserves data showing total official reserves of $125,516 million USD as of March 23, 2026, up $358 million from the prior week but down $2,614 million from February 27, 2026. The reserves composition includes $66,316 million in U.S. dollars (53% of total) and $29,454 million in other foreign currencies.
key_points: Total official international reserves: $125,516 million USD (March 23, 2026)
Weekly change: +$358 million; change from Feb 27, 2026: -$2,614 million
Gold holdings: $0 (Canada holds no gold in official reserves)
why_it_matters: International reserves data provides insight into a central bank's foreign exchange liquidity position and its capacity to intervene in currency markets. For Canada, this weekly snapshot shows the BoC's reserve adequacy. However, this data is low-frequency, backward-looking, and has minimal direct market impact compared to high-tier macro releases like CPI or employment data.
macro_context: Canada's international reserves declined $2.6 billion over the approximately three-week period from late February to late March 2026. The reserves composition shows Canada's portfolio is heavily weighted toward USD (53%) with moderate holdings of other currencies and SDRs. This data is a technical accounting release with no stated policy implications.

issues_found: Source is a thin technical data table with no analytical content, commentary, or policy context

Record quality is accurate but lacks substance beyond the data table itself
